_comment: Карта пропускаемых тестов CommonMark по версиям. Пропуск делается через аннотацию &Выключен в OneUnit. Каждая запись объясняет ПОЧЕМУ тест пропущен (известное архитектурное ограничение или сложный edge case). 632/652 тестов проходят активно (96.9%). Чтобы реализовать оставшиеся 20, нужна полноценная container-model с open-blocks stack (CM §4.7) и proper delimiter-stack для скобок с link-in-link deactivation.

examples.5: Tabs inside list items — tab expansion at container boundary (CM §2.2). Needs proper container line prefix processing.
examples.6: Tabs inside block quotes — tab expansion across `>` marker boundary.
examples.7: Tabs inside list items — double-tab content indent calculation.
examples.93: Setext underline in block-quote lazy continuation. CM disallows setext underline from being created by a lazy continuation line.
examples.236: Block quote followed by indented-code-style line: CM treats outside content as separate indented code block. Our recursive BQ collects via lazy continuation.
examples.237: Fenced code inside block quote that doesn't close: closing fence must also carry `>` prefix; without it the fence stays open and ends at BQ boundary.
examples.307: Loose-list detection in deeply nested lists when blank line follows nested content.
examples.312: Lazy continuation of list item paragraph when continuation line looks like a list marker at higher indent.
examples.318: Loose-vs-tight detection when blank lines are *inside* a fenced code block — they should not make the surrounding list loose.
examples.319: Loose-list paragraph-extension after blank inside nested list item.
examples.518: Link-in-link deactivation rule (CM §6.3): a `[` opener inside a resolved link must be deactivated; our recursive bracket scanner allows them.
examples.519: Same link-in-link rule + emphasis around outer brackets.
examples.520: Image (`![…]`) with nested resolved links — same deactivation rule.
examples.524: Raw HTML attribute value crossing a `](url)` boundary — bracket scanner must respect HTML attribute quoting.
examples.526: Autolink `<https://…>` whose body contains `](uri)` — autolink scanner must consume past brackets.
examples.533: Nested link inside emphasis inside reference link.
examples.536: HTML attribute crossing bracket + reference resolution.
examples.540: Unicode case-folding for reference labels (`ß` → `SS`). Our normalizer only does ASCII case lowering.
